title: Ajit Ranade: India’s stubbornly high PF payout rate is getting in the way of its monetary policy
description: The EPFO’s 8.25% payout is 3 percentage points above the Reserve Bank of India’s policy rate. For RBI’s credit easing to take effect, the PF rate must be linked in some way with rates in the larger savings market. When will India’s political economy let this distortion end?
